name: Hangzhou
intro: Hangzhou fits travelers who want a scenic break from megacities, with lakeside walks, tea culture, and an easy side trip from Shanghai.
seoTitle: Hangzhou Travel Guide for First-Time Visitors
seoDescription: Plan a first trip to Hangzhou with West Lake itinerary ideas, hotel-base advice, and realistic pacing for a day trip or overnight stay.
tripPace: Best when the itinerary slows down and leaves space for scenery.
highlights: A strong scenic counterweight to faster megacity travel, especially for travelers who want room to breathe.
Works as either a soft overnight stop or a carefully shaped extension from Shanghai.
Best appreciated when scenery, tea culture, and pacing matter more than attraction density.

planningPriorities.title: Decide day trip or overnight first
planningPriorities.description: That single choice changes the pace, hotel need, and how much of the lake area can be enjoyed well.

planningPriorities.title: Avoid treating the city like a checklist
planningPriorities.description: Hangzhou is most rewarding when the trip allows for walking, views, and unhurried movement.

planningPriorities.title: Fit Hangzhou into the larger route thoughtfully
planningPriorities.description: This city shines most when used to soften a more intense multi-city trip.

transportNote: Hangzhou is easiest to use as a softer scenic stop when rail timing and the Shanghai connection are planned before the final itinerary is fixed.
